item.author: @Ronald_vanLoon

item.title: The recommendations are good as far as they go, and as recently as four years ago I would have been ...
item.description:
The recommendations are good as far as they go, and as recently as four years ago I would have been thrilled with such a report. However, due to the explosive rise of AI, many of the report’s goals seem moot or even quaint now. In particular, we are rapidly approaching the point where *human* creation of new knowledge will be a minor asterisk compared to all the vast amounts of knowledge that AI will be able to generate. The report mentions, in passing, the challenges that AI poses in the classroom, and the need for AI-literate education. But these are still very minor considerations, and fail to fully grasp the big picture of how the nature of knowledge work is about to get radically transformed. Unfortunately, this report just adds to my growing resignation that the vast majority of academics *really* don’t get it when it comes to the rise of AI.
3/4
--- @tunguz | ❤️ 2 | 🔁 0 | 💬 2 | 👁 345
item.author: @tunguz

item.title: I just came across this Yale report on trust in Higher Education. The report confirms what many of u...
item.description:
I just came across this Yale report on trust in Higher Education. The report confirms what many of us have known for a while: the trust in higher ed is low and only getting lower. Public confidence in higher education fell from 57% a decade ago to 36% in 2024, and even after a slight rebound in 2025, 70% of Americans still said higher education was headed in the wrong direction. The report says the main drivers are high cost and doubts about value, opaque admissions, campus speech and political-bias concerns, self-censorship, plus internal problems such as grade inflation, technology-driven distraction, and bureaucratic growth. Its deeper diagnosis is mission drift: universities have tried to be “all things to all people,” which has made it harder for the public to know what they are for or whether they are succeeding.
1/4
--- @tunguz | ❤️ 5 | 🔁 0 | 💬 1 | 👁 529
item.author: @tunguz
